expectations.evidence: skill_output.md contains encode_payload as a central node consuming PayloadHandlePP and ProducerConfig, calling select_serializer, serialize_proto, transmit_message, and dispatch_event.
skill_output.md contains both serialize_proto (consumes Envelope, produces RawBytes) and serialize_wsdl (consumes LegacyEvent_CC, produces RawBytes).

expectations.text: Diagram contains at least 2 nodes from typedef chain (RawBytes, MessageBody, Payload, PayloadHandle)
expectations.evidence: skill_output.md contains all 5 typedef chain nodes: RawBytes, MessageBody, Payload, PayloadHandle, PayloadHandlePP with correct typedef_of edges between them.
skill_output.md contains both transmit_message (produces/consumes RawBytes) and dispatch_event (calls EventBus).

expectations.text: Tool call log exists and is non-empty
expectations.evidence: tool_calls.json exists with 7 entries covering: producer node extraction, typedef_of edges, call chain tracing, proto path, WSDL path, LegacyEvent maps_to, and EventBus connection.

strengths: Full typedef chain captured: RawBytes -> MessageBody -> Payload -> PayloadHandle -> PayloadHandlePP with typedef_of edges
Proto path correctly shown: encode_payload -> serialize_proto -> consumes Envelope -> produces RawBytes
WSDL path correctly shown: handle_transmitting -> build_legacy_event -> LegacyEvent_CC -> serialize_wsdl -> RawBytes
LegacyEvent maps_to bridge present: LegacyEvent_CC maps_to LegacyEvent_Proto maps_to LegacyEvent_WSDL (from maps_to edges in graph)
ProducerConfig as control input to encode_payload and select_serializer shown
gaps: CORRECTION: ptr_depth IS stored on typedef_of edges (verified: values 0-3 present in graph JSON). Earlier gap analysis was wrong.
select_serializer: final agent found it DOES have calls edges to both serialize_proto and serialize_wsdl — graph encodes both branches. Earlier analysis missed this.
Envelope defined in proto/messages.proto (not producer files) — requires cross-file type tracing

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks for LegacyEvent_CC or the maps_to bridge — this is a key CodeGrapher feature and should be explicitly asserted in iteration 2.
Add assertion for ptr_depth presence on typedef edges — it IS in the graph, so this should pass and serves as a useful regression test.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong pass. Graph captures more than initially assessed: ptr_depth on typedef edges confirmed, select_serializer branching confirmed. Parser quality higher than first estimated.
